event_name: 15 Malicious JetBrains Plugins Stole AI API Keys from 70,000 Developers
confidence_reason: JetBrains official response confirms the purge and disablement path; StepSecurity provides the plugin inventory, publisher handles, timeline, and C2 details.

artifact_analysis.execution_trigger: developer enters an AI provider API key and clicks Apply inside the plugin settings
payload_behavior: posts JSON payloads over plaintext HTTP
installs a JVM-wide X509TrustManager to suppress TLS warnings

provenance.jetbrains_response: official vendor purge and disablement notice
provenance.stepsecurity_research: plugin inventory, publisher handles, C2 analysis, and timeline

process_patterns: JetBrains IDE process sending HTTP POST requests to 39.107.60.51

filesystem_hunts: JetBrains plugin directories containing the listed plugin IDs
process_hunts: IDE process telemetry showing outbound HTTP to 39.107.60.51
network_hunts: proxy or firewall logs showing POST /api/software/key or /api/software/check from 39.107.60.51
ci_cd_hunts: not_applicable unless the same AI keys were reused in build automation
registry_hunts: Marketplace inventory for the 15 malicious plugin IDs

open_questions: What exact repository or publisher recovery path produced the 7 vendor accounts?
How many AI API keys were actually exfiltrated versus merely exposed to the C2 endpoint?
Did any of the affected developers reuse the stolen AI keys in other environments?

defender_takeaways.detection: Watch JetBrains IDE telemetry for the listed plugin IDs, the publisher handles, and outbound HTTP to 39.107.60.51.
defender_takeaways.hunting: Scope IDE settings, plugin directories, proxy logs, and AI-provider billing records before removing all evidence.
defender_takeaways.remediation: Remove the plugins, block the C2 IP, and rotate any AI provider keys entered into the affected settings screens.
defender_takeaways.prevention: Require Marketplace allowlists and monitor IDE extensions with the same rigor used for browser add-ons or package managers.

containment_complete: Listed plugins removed from all managed JetBrains IDEs
recovery_complete: AI provider keys entered into the plugins revoked and reissued
Provider usage logs show no post-discovery abuse
closure_required: No listed plugin IDs remain installed
Risk owner accepts any residual uncertainty
